activities_projects.title: Mapping the INC Sessions
activities_projects.objective: Understand the geographical spread and strategic shifts of the Congress
steps: Locate the three critical Congress sessions: Calcutta (Sept 1920), Nagpur (Dec 1920), and Madras (1927)
Identify and mark the 'Purna Swaraj' resolution site (Lahore 1929)
Write a brief note next to each location explaining what was decided there
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Spatial understanding of political decision-making and planning
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of locations and historical significance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Non-Cooperation vs. Civil Disobedience: A Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between the methods and scales of the two major movements
steps: List methods unique to Non-Cooperation in one circle (e.g., surrendering titles)
List methods unique to Civil Disobedience in the other (e.g., breaking laws)
Fill the intersection with shared Gandhian principles like Ahimsa and Satyagraha
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Conceptual clarity on the evolution of protest strategies
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical classification and depth of points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Simon Go Back' Campaign Storyboard
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the reasons for the 1928 boycott
steps: Draw a 3-part storyboard: 1. The arrival of the Commission, 2. The protest with black flags, 3. The reason for boycott (absence of Indians)
Include a caption explaining why the 'All-White' commission was seen as an insult to India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands constitutional crises and mass mobilization
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of historical narrative and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bharat Mata: Symbolism Audit
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct how allegories create a sense of collective belonging
materials: Printout of Abanindranath Tagore’s Bharat Mata
steps: Observe the attributes: the beads, the book, the cloth, and the sheaves of grain
Explain how these symbols aimed to portray India as a provider of 'spiritual' and 'material' wealth
Research how this image differs from the later 'militant' versions of Bharat Mata
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analysis of the role of icons in building national identity

activities_projects.title: The Awadh Peasant Movement Fact-File
activities_projects.objective: Examine the rural dimensions of nationalism
steps: Identify the leader (Baba Ramchandra) and the grievances (Begar/High Rents)
Explain the formation of the Oudh Kisan Sabha
Discuss why the Congress leaders were unhappy when peasants turned to violence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that different groups had different interpretations of 'Swaraj'

activities_projects.title: The Dandi March: Strategic Route Map
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the logistics and outreach of the Salt Satyagraha
steps: Trace the 240-mile route from Sabarmati to Dandi
Highlight key stops where Gandhi spoke to people
List why salt was chosen as a unifying symbol for both rich and poor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the concept of salt as a powerful political tool
activities_projects.evaluation: Mapping accuracy and strategic analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rowlatt Act & Jallianwala Bagh: Cause-Effect Chain
activities_projects.objective: Trace the escalation of tension in 1919
steps: Create a chain: Passage of Rowlatt Act -> Peaceful Hartals -> Martial Law -> Jallianwala Bagh Incident -> Non-Cooperation
Write a brief 'Why' for each arrow in the chain
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding historical causality and turning points
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow and factual accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Folklore and Identity: A Collection Project
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of history and fiction in nationalism
steps: Find a folk tale or ballad from your own region used to instill pride during the colonial era
Write a summary of the story and its moral lesson
Explain how 're-discovering' history helped counter British narratives
activities_projects.evaluation: Authenticity and relevance of the folklore (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Women in the Civil Disobedience Movement
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the participation of women in urban and rural areas
steps: Research the role of Sarojini Naidu or women in general during the salt picketing
Contrast the participation of high-caste urban women with rich peasant rural women
Explain how their involvement changed their perception of service to the nation

activities_projects.title: The 'Poona Pact' Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Examine the internal challenges to a unified national identity
steps: Identify the two viewpoints regarding reserved seats for Dalits
Outline the compromise reached between Gandhiji and Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
Summarize how the Pact balanced unity and specific group rights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Insights into the social complexities of the movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced representation of both viewpoints (10 marks)
